questions.question: Explain the 'Variety' characteristic of Big Data and provide two examples of data types it encompasses.
expectedPoints: Definition: Variety refers to the different types and formats of data sources.
Includes unstructured data (e.g., text documents, images, videos).

questions.question: Describe the roles of the NameNode and DataNodes in the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS). How does this architecture provide fault tolerance?
expectedPoints: NameNode: Master server; manages filesystem metadata (namespace, block locations). Does not store actual data.
DataNodes: Slave nodes; store the actual data blocks and serve read/write requests.
Fault Tolerance: Achieved through data replication. Each block is replicated across multiple DataNodes (default is 3).
If a DataNode fails, the NameNode initiates replication of its blocks from another replica to maintain the replication factor.
This ensures data is not lost and remains available even during hardware failures.
